project: TOPOS: Topology-Aware AllReduce Selection with Formal Verification and Uncertainty Quantification

claims.claim: CV accuracy 99.9% with LOFO gap of 3.6pp on all features
Cost-model argmin baseline achieves 100% accuracy because labels are derived from cost model

evidence.result: 100% accuracy, 0 mean regret. Labels come from _contention_adjusted_cost, so argmin trivially matches.
evidence.note: This is honestly reported as a limitation of evaluating on analytically generated ground truth

claims.claim: Feature importance: cost-model features 84.4%, graph-structural 11.8%, TDA 3.9%

evidence.result: GBM feature importance: base_cost_model 0.8438, graph_structural 0.1176, tda_topological 0.0386

claims.claim: Counterexample-guided active learning loop improves Z3 verification rate from 68.3% to 98.3% in 2 iterations

evidence.result: Iteration 0: 68.3% (19 counterexamples), Iteration 1: 98.3% (1), Iteration 2: 98.3% (1, converged). 21 total counterexamples across 10 topology configs.
evidence.note: Terminology: this is counterexample-guided active learning, not CEGAR. We operate on point predictions, not a finite abstraction lattice.

claims.reproduce: python3 -c "from smt_analysis import run_verification_experiment; import json; print(json.dumps(run_verification_experiment(), indent=2))"

claims.claim: Dataset expanded to 1,842 instances across 175 unique topologies, 2-128 nodes

evidence.result: 1842 entries, 175 topologies, max 128 nodes. Distribution: dragonfly 486, fat-tree 384, multi-node 312, heterogeneous 300, torus 240, single-switch 120.

claims.claim: Z3 is ~10,000x slower than RF inference (1.5ms vs 0.15us), justifying ML for runtime decisions

evidence.result: Z3 avg: 1.5ms, RF avg: 0.15us, speedup ~10,000x (exact ratio varies by run, typically 10,000-16,000x)

claims.reproduce: python3 -c "from smt_analysis import run_cegar_experiment; r = run_cegar_experiment(); print(f'Z3: {r[\"z3_avg_time_ms\"]:.1f}ms, RF: {r[\"rf_avg_time_ms\"]*1000:.2f}us, ratio: {r[\"z3_vs_rf_speedup\"]:.0f}x')"

claims.claim: Accuracy >97.6% at all scale bands from 2 to 128 nodes
Cross-scale generalization (train <=16, test >32) achieves 88.9%

evidence.result: 88.9% accuracy when training only on topologies with <=16 nodes and testing on >32 nodes

claims.claim: Per-family LOFO: torus 100%, dragonfly 97.9%, multi-node 96.5%, fat-tree 95.3%, heterogeneous 90.7%, single-switch 78.3%

evidence.result: All five hierarchical families exceed 90% LOFO accuracy. Single-switch improved from 0% to 78.3% via feature pruning.

claims.claim: Bias-variance decomposition: bias 0.72%, variance 1.05%, total error 1.77%

evidence.result: Bias 0.0072, variance 0.0105, total 0.0177. Previously bias was 80.8% of error; now variance-dominated.

claims.claim: Recursive halving-doubling is provably optimal for M in [1, 1GB] on uniform-bandwidth 8-GPU topologies

evidence.result: UNSAT certificate — no algorithm beats rec_halving across entire range

claims.reproduce: python3 -c "from smt_analysis import smt_verify_optimality, TopologyParams; p=TopologyParams(8,600,600); print(smt_verify_optimality('rec_halving',p,(1,1073741824)))"

claims.claim: Cost algebra properties verified: monotonicity (100%), bandwidth dominance (100%), contention monotonicity (100%)

evidence.result: All monotonicity and bandwidth dominance properties hold analytically

claims.claim: 49 graph-structured features capture relational topology information: Laplacian spectrum, WL kernels, motifs, neighborhood aggregation
Ring AllReduce is bandwidth-optimal on single-link-per-node topologies (Theorem 1)

evidence.smt_verification: smt_analysis.py verifies numerically and via Z3

claims.claim: Hierarchical ring saves bandwidth when rho > 1 (Theorem 2)

evidence.note: Originally stated as rho > 1+1/(k-1); corrected to rho > 1 after fixing algebraic error that dropped (k-1)/k factor

claims.claim: Counterexample-guided active learning loop verification rate is monotonically non-decreasing across iterations

evidence.proof_sketch: Each counterexample replaces ML prediction with analytically optimal algorithm. Z3 will verify the optimal algorithm (UNSAT), so V_i subset V_{i+1}. Convergence in at most N=|T|*|M| iterations. This is counterexample-guided active learning, not CEGAR.

claims.claim: Contention penalty applies only to bandwidth term, not latency

evidence.experimental_validation: Bandwidth-only contention: 60.1% accuracy vs both-terms contention: 32.0% (-28.1pp), validating decomposition

claims.claim: Feature pruning removes 11-19 noise features per family

evidence.result: single_switch: 19, torus: 19, heterogeneous: 14, multi_node: 12, fat_tree: 12, dragonfly: 11

claims.claim: All test-set errors concentrate in single-switch family at 1-8 nodes, with max regret 9.8%

evidence.result: 2 errors out of 369 test instances, both in single_switch. Mean regret on errors: 5.5%, max regret: 9.8%. Zero regret for 99.5% of instances.

claims.claim: Conformal prediction sets are singletons for 100% of test instances at alpha=0.10

evidence.result: Mean set size 1.000, singleton fraction 1.0, empirical coverage 1.0. Indicates high classifier certainty on this dataset.

claims.claim: Dataset size ablation: 460 samples achieve 99.8% CV, diminishing returns beyond ~500 samples
Model ablation: RF 100%, GBM 99.9%, stacking 99.8%, LogReg 98.6% on all features

evidence.result: RF achieves 100% CV, GBM 99.9%, stacking ensemble 99.8%, LogReg 98.6%

non_claims: All results are simulation-based; no hardware validation has been performed
The DES simulator is NOT calibrated against hardware
NCCL baselines are modeled approximations, not measurements of the actual NCCL library
Contention model parameters are heuristic, not formally verified
Compositional reasoning for cyclic topologies (torus) is NOT formally sound
The counterexample-guided active learning loop is NOT classical CEGAR: it operates on point predictions, not a finite abstraction lattice
Cost-model argmin achieves 100% accuracy because the ground-truth labels are derived from the same cost model — this is a limitation of evaluation on analytically generated data
TDA features contribute only 3.9% of feature importance; their value would be greater on hardware-measured ground truth where cost models are imperfect
Conformal prediction sets are all singletons at alpha=0.10, indicating the classification problem is 'easy' for the model on this dataset
TOPOS selects among 6 existing algorithms — it does not synthesize new collective schedules
The alpha-beta cost model is linear in M (QF_LRA), making Z3 verification trivially decidable
Soundness gap: verification is against the alpha-beta model but training labels come from the contention-aware model; models disagree on 17.6% of instances
Neuro-symbolic integration is sequential (symbolic -> neural -> symbolic) with no gradient-level feedback between components

reproducibility.verification: python3 -c "from smt_analysis import run_verification_experiment; import json; print(json.dumps(run_verification_experiment(), indent=2))"
reproducibility.smt_rules: python3 -c "from smt_analysis import run_comprehensive_smt_analysis; import json; r = run_comprehensive_smt_analysis(); print(json.dumps(r['_summary'], indent=2))"
